title: The Bible in Context
subtitle: The real world behind the sacred text
author: Wordsus Team
description: A fascinating journey through the ancient world to understand the historical, cultural and geographical context of the Scriptures. Discover how biblical characters lived, thought and felt to transform your reading of the Bible.

chapters.title: Abraham's Journey and Middle Eastern Culture
chapters.description: Explore the life of Abraham and how his nomadic environment defined his big decisions. Understanding this context transforms our reading of the book of Genesis.

chapters.title: The geographical and spiritual significance of the desert
chapters.description: The desert is not just sand, it is an unforgiving region that transforms leaders. We analyze why large testing processes occur in these lands.

chapters.title: Origin and identity of the Pharisees in ancient times
chapters.description: Beyond being antagonists, the Pharisees were highly respected popular leaders. Discover how they emerged and why their theology clashed so much with the Lord.

chapters.title: The sacred law of hospitality in the ancient world
chapters.description: Denying asylum to a stranger was a very serious cultural crime in that region. Learn how this rule worked and why it explains surprising stories.

chapters.title: Biblical economics and the real value of the ancient denarius
chapters.description: Understanding money in ancient times changes the parables profoundly. Discover the purchasing power of a denarius and the abysmal value of a talent.

chapters.title: Moses before the Egyptian empire and its formal formation
chapters.description: Education in Egypt prepared Moses to lead an entire nation to Canaan. We analyze the clash between the luxury of the pharaoh and harsh slavery.

chapters.title: The Sea of ​​Galilee and its influence on the life of Jesus
chapters.description: This iconic lake was the main setting for multiple miracles. We explore its geography, sudden violent storms and the culture of its coasts.

chapters.title: The Babylonian Empire and the impact of the great exile
chapters.description: The painful deportation to Babylon was Israel's greatest historical trauma. Learn how this power transformed religious identity and writing.

chapters.title: Structure and purpose of the tabernacle in the desert
chapters.description: This sacred tent organized the nomadic life of the Israelites on their journey. We detail its areas, the materials and the symbolism of the sacrifices.

chapters.title: David and the geopolitical transition of the kingdom of Israel
chapters.description: From outcast shepherd to unifying king, David changed the military history of his nation. We analyze the conquest of Jerusalem and the strategic alliances.

chapters.title: The hard job of being a sheepherder in Judea
chapters.description: Forget the traditional romantic image. Herding was rough, lonely and dangerous work. Understanding it gives new weight and meaning to Psalm twenty-three.

chapters.title: The fishing industry during the first century in Judea
chapters.description: The fishermen were not very poor farmers, they ran profitable businesses under heavy taxes. We explore their daily routine and position in society.

chapters.title: Sacred geography and the natural defense of Jerusalem
chapters.description: Located on various mountains and surrounded by deep valleys, the city was a natural fortress. Discover why all the pilgrims had to climb it.

chapters.title: The political and social context during the life of Jesus
chapters.description: Jesus was born in a true political powder keg strictly dominated by Rome. We analyze social tensions, taxes and expectations of liberation.

chapters.title: The Roman Empire and its strict control over Judea
chapters.description: Rome's great military machine dictated every aspect of daily life. Learn about the prefects, the legions, and the balance of power with the Sanhedrin.

chapters.title: Peter of Galilee and his role in the beginning of the movement
chapters.description: An impulsive man and fisherman by trade who became a great leader. We study his personal evolution when facing the strong Greco-Roman culture.

chapters.title: Marriage and spousal contracts at the time
chapters.description: The Jewish engagement was legally binding, much stricter than a modern courtship. Understanding this custom clarifies the risk that Mary took.

chapters.title: The celebration of Passover and the sacrifice of the lamb
chapters.description: The biggest festival on the calendar attracted thousands of pilgrims without exception. We detail the ritual dinner, the festive atmosphere and the surveillance of the troops.

chapters.title: Elijah against the political influence of the cult of Baal
chapters.description: The extensive drought was not a simple ecological disaster, it was a war against the Canaanite god of rain. Understand the challenge on the great Mount Carmel.

chapters.title: Ritual purity and the social stigma of ancient leprosy
chapters.description: Being declared unclean implied total expulsion from civil and religious society. We analyze strict health laws and the work of the priest.
